{{Infobox artifact
| name = Tyrfang
| introduced = ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea
}}

''See also: [[Tyrfang Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Tyrfang, also known as the executioner's blade, is a legendary [[sword]] that existed before the establishment of the [[Empire]]. The weapon features a black metal blade marked by veins of bright red light that resemble flowing blood. It possesses an incredibly potent and hostile [[aura]], characterized by a sense of overwhelming dread, despair, and macabre madness. This corrupting intent is so powerful that it can cause the ground to die, peel away stone, or paralyze those nearby with a feeling of inescapable death. The blade's history is deeply violent, and its power is so intense that it can shatter enemies or cause bystanders to collapse in fear. [[Jarelys Teach]], the [[Head of the Imperial Guard]], serves as the [[sword]]'s current wielder and [[Vessel]]. She earned the right to bond with Tyrfang by surviving its lethal intent, a feat made possible by her having the heart of a [[Bonereaver]]. While she cannot empower the weapon through magic like a [[Magister]], her focused intent works in tandem with the [[sword]]'s own power. The weapon is noted to recognize its creator, and it was once part of a lineage of blades that included a more corrupt, original prototype used for pure destruction.}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Tyrfang]] is an executioner's blade that predates the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c7">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 7</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Tyrfang is a black metal [[sword]] characterized by rough textures and veins of bright red that radiate a hostile intent. Also known as the Executioner’s Blade, this weapon possesses an [[aura]] capable of striking humans dead and can release a corrosive dark power. Held by [[Teach]], the blade is closely associated with the specific intent used by its wielder. It is believed that [[Jorin Maze-walker]] was likely the one who [[Awakened]] the weapon, which was previously carried by an individual who provided [[Calder]] with a tour.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is a black [[sword]] that radiates hostile [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is a [[sword]] made of rough black metal with veins of bright red.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The bearer of [[Tyrfang]] has given [[Calder]] a tour.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is a weapon with an [[aura]] that can strike humans dead.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c10">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is also referred to as the Executioner’s Blade.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is a weapon held by [[Teach]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is a blade that can release dark power.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]]'s power has corrosive [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is associated with the [[Intent]] used by [[Teach]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c18">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]] is a weapon that [[Jorin Maze-walker]] likely [[Awakened]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c20">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|Tyrfang is a legendary executioner's blade characterized by its black metal and a channel of red light. When [[Teach]] picked up the weapon, it became her vessel, serving as an object carried by a bearer. The [[sword]] emanates a sense of dread and possesses lethal intent. During combat, the blade releases a red-tinted shadow when it is used.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]] is an executioner's blade with lethal [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c14">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]] is a [[sword]] that [[Teach]] picked up and which became her [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]] is a black-and-red blade that emanates dread.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]] is an object carried by a bearer.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c19">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]] releases a red-tinted shadow when used.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c20">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]] is a legendary weapon with a black blade and a red light channel.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c21">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Tyrfang is a legendary [[sword]] belonging to [[General Teach]]. While it remains a sheathed weapon, it is currently carried by [[Kern]]. The blade itself is rotten and corrupting, possessing enough power to blight entire forests with every single cut. Furthermore, the weapon is characterized by its intrusive [[Intent]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]] is [[General Teach]]'s legendary [[sword]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c1">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]] is a legendary [[sword]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]] is a sheathed weapon.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]] is carried by [[Kern]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]]'s blade is rotten and corrupting.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]] holds enough power to blight forests with every cut.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Tyrfang]] has intrusive [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Tyrfang]] blazes with black power streaked with blood and brings a sense of corruption.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Tyrfang]] has a dark [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Tyrfang]] is a weapon with noxious [[Intent]] that can corrupt stone.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Tyrfang]] granted power to the [[Heart of Nakothi]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c19">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Artifacts]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire artifacts]]
